name: R-CNN: Region-based Convolutional Neural Networks
description:
Architecture Overview

R-CNN (Regions with CNN features) revolutionized object detection by combining region proposals with deep convolutional neural networks. The process involves four main stages: Region Proposal (generating candidate boxes), Feature Extraction (extracting attributes via CNN), Classification (identifying objects via SVM), and Bounding Box Regression (refining object location).

Key Components:

  • Selective Search: A grouping algorithm that finds likely object locations.
  • Warping: Resizes variable-sized regions into a fixed input size (224x224) for the CNN.
  • CNN: Acts as a feature extractor, producing a high-dimensional vector.
  • Linear SVMs: One per category to classify the extracted features.
  • Bbox Regressor: A linear model that learns to shift and scale the proposal to fit the ground truth better.
code:
graph TD
  Start[Input Image] --> SS[Selective Search Algorithm]
  SS --> |~2000 Proposals| ROI[Region Proposals / ROIs]
  ROI --> Warp[Warping & Resizing - 224x224]
  Warp --> CNN[Deep CNN - Feature Extractor]
  CNN --> Features[Feature Vector - 4096-d]

  subgraph DetectionHead [Classification & Refinement Stage]
    Features --> SVM[Linear SVMs - Class Specific]
    Features --> BBR[Bounding Box Regressors]
  end

  SVM --> Out1[Class Labels]
  BBR --> Out2[Refined Bounding Boxes]
  
  Out1 --> Final[Non-Maximum Suppression]
  Out2 --> Final
  Final --> End[Final Detected Objects]

  style DetectionHead fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
  classDef default fill:#ffffff,stroke:#334155,stroke-width:2px,color:#000000
  classDef highlight fill:#e2e8f0,stroke:#1e293b,stroke-width:2px,color:#000000
  class Start,End highlight
